trainees.name: Michael Wilson
trainees.current_summary: Senior trainee acting as a de facto coach. On May 15 Brent had him serve as the drop-toe-hold giver for the whole face-bump block — Michael coached Jack through his first take, and Brent had Michael demonstrate correct running form alongside him when Dalton was flagged for crooked running. On his own face bumps, no notes — including the Jimmy spot-check. On May 12 walked Brandon through his first-ever spot one and ran Jack's first promo cleanly. Continues to draw zero flags on his own work.

trainees.name: Hailey Stokely
trainees.current_summary: Veteran trainee operating at near-trainer level. Not at May 15. May 12 she effectively co-ran the spot-one block — coached Jack through every unfamiliar piece (lockup-to-headlock transition, leapfrog form including where to bend and how low, hip toss form including foot placement and jump mechanics), then drilled the jumper role on the leapfrog with him (her first time as jumper) until she felt comfortable. Hit spot one flawlessly at full speed with Jack, then volunteered to run it a second time with Jeff to balance the odd numbers — also clean. Ran the second-rope hurricanrana on Jimmy on the turnbuckle and worked satisfaction with Michael. One self-inflicted clip on her first frog splash off the second rope — hand placement was slightly off. Caught DC's head dropping during the headlock, and shut down his promo cleanly with Jimmy when he crossed a personal-insult line. Currently locking in the hurricanrana and a satisfaction ahead of the next TAW show.

trainees.name: Jacob Justice
trainees.notes: Marked inactive May 12, 2026 after six consecutive absences from April 7 onward. Not expected to return to training.
trainees.current_summary: Selling visibly improved through early spring, but he hasn't been at a session since April 7 — six consecutive absences and counting. Confidence and pacing were the biggest gaps when he was active: flagged as timid and slow on rope-throw drills on April 7, and flagged as an injury risk for landing elbow-first on bumps. Hard to track progress with this much absence.

trainees.name: Tyler Skelton
trainees.current_summary: Big-frame trainee still learning how his height and size shape his ringwork. May 15 had a clean lockup with Jeff (no notes) and a believable one with Michael, plus a clean run as the drop-toe-hold giver with Brandon after some execution pointers from Jimmy on the second attempt. The flag from this session was on the face-bump receiving end — he was using his hands too much to catch himself during the drop-toe-hold face bump, and Joey read it as mostly psychological. Jimmy's face-bump spot-check after the initial drills was clean for him. May 8: handled front rolls cleanly after Cody form coaching, took the kick + back forearm with no notes. German suplex came together cleanly on April 24. Not at May 12.

trainees.name: Jay Taylor
trainees.notes: Removed from program April 14, 2026. Blackballed from local wrestling scene due to unsafe work, lack of attendance, refusal to accept criticism.

trainees.name: DC
trainees.notes: Removed from the training program May 15, 2026 by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote following the May 12 promo incident (personal insults toward Hailey). Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel determined Wrestler's Court was the most impartial way to handle the decision. DC pled his case in front of the trainees; each trainee voted in the locker room with reasoning. DC's defense involved disturbing allegations — locker-room vote notes kept separately at Joey's call, not in this record. Prior conduct flag: May 12 promo, called Hailey a bitch, crossed other personal lines.
trainees.current_summary: Removed from the program by unanimous Wrestler's Court vote on May 15, 2026. He sat out the front of the May 15 session in a meeting with Jimmy, Cody, and Kytel about the May 12 promo incident, then pled his case to the trainees before the vote. Technical record before removal: form fundamentals still needed polish at the end — stiff lockups, head dropping during the headlock, drifting on the two-person rope-crossing drill, timing/coordination off on his first spot-one attempt on May 12. The decisive issue was conduct, not technique: in promos opposite Hailey on May 12 he tried to make her go first 'because she's a woman' despite her being the heel, then in his own promo got too heated and called her a bitch among other personal lines. Jimmy and Hailey stopped the promo on the spot and made clear that personal insults were a hard line; Wrestler's Court three days later closed the program out.

trainees.name: Dalton
trainees.current_summary: The most coaching-intensive trainee in the program right now, and May 15 fit that pattern almost end to end — corrected on hand placement during his lockup with Tyler, multiple corrections on face-bump form during drop toe holds with Michael, flagged for crooked running (Michael and Brent demonstrated correct form before a better third attempt), then drilled turnbuckle running for refinement after coaching on how to sell being thrown into the corner. On the trading drop-toe-hold drill he got leg-placement pointers as the giver with Nick, much better on the second attempt, and ran a couple of clean reps giving to Kirkland after. **The bright spot of his session: when Jimmy came out to spot-check face bumps, Dalton got an explicit positive flag for good execution** — first piece of unprompted praise from Jimmy in his record. No notes on hip tosses after Brent's walk-through. Returned after missing May 8 and May 12.

trainees.name: Jack
trainees.notes: Age 14. Non-bumping restriction lifted May 5, 2026 — parent always present at training.
trainees.current_summary: Eight weeks in and continuing on a fast track. May 15 was a heavy first-time session: first time on the headlock-to-wrist-control drill (Brent walked him slowly through it, then he picked it up quickly), first time taking a drop toe hold from Michael (clean after the coaching pass), first time giving a drop toe hold to Nick (clean on the second attempt with refinement reps that all looked good). Got a hesitation note on his first hip toss, much better on the next rep. Kirkland coached him on foot placement in his lockup with Brent — after the note everything looked great — and Brent flagged his lockup with Nick as looking really good. Got a leg-placement coaching point on his initial face bump too. May 12 was first time on lockup-to-headlock transition, first time as the jumper's base on a leapfrog, first time as the hip-toss thrower, first promo. Pattern holds: brief coaching, then clean execution.

trainees.name: Nick
trainees.notes: Jack's dad. Officially joined the program April 14, 2026.
trainees.current_summary: Six sessions in and still the easiest trainee in the gym to coach. May 15 went almost note-free across every block: Brent coached him and Jeff on looking strong in the lockup-to-corner push and both looked good after; clean run through the headlock drill; no notes on the initial face bump; took the drop toe hold from Michael clean after a coaching pass; volunteered to take from Jeff (Jeff's first time giving) and Jeff did well on it; gave a drop toe hold himself with no execution issues; clean as the hip-toss recipient. The May 12 spot-one timing/coordination issues paired with DC were not specifically called out as Nick's. Continues to absorb corrections on the first rep.

trainees.name: Brandon
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Has been training for a while and has worked a few matches at TAW.
trainees.current_summary: Three sessions in. May 15 was almost entirely clean — Kirkland coached him on making the lockup struggle look realistic in either direction (one of his first lockups), and from there everything ran clean: clean drop-toe-hold form and execution taking from Michael, clean face bump in Jimmy's spot-check, clean as the drop-toe-hold giver with Jeff. No notes on the headlock drill from Brent. May 12 was his first time on spot one — Michael walked him through the leapfrog and up-and-over and Jimmy commended him directly. The earlier May 12 form flags (tailbone landing on bumps, drifting on the rope-crossing drill) did not repeat on May 15. Joining from Cody's roster with TAW match experience tracks with how quickly he absorbs new spots.

trainees.name: Jeff
trainees.notes: Joined the program May 5, 2026 from Cody McCulley's personal roster. Relatively new to training.
trainees.current_summary: Four sessions in and consistently easy to coach. May 15 was almost note-free in the early going: Brent coached him and Nick on looking strong in the lockup-to-corner push, both looked good after; clean run through the headlock drill (no notes); good first drop-toe-hold attempt with just a small forearm-placement note; gave drop toe holds to Nick (Nick volunteered to take from him) and did well. The one real flag of the session was on hip tosses — he flipped early on his first attempt, then subsequent attempts came together after posting and placement coaching. May 12: clean run through spot one with Hailey (his first time on the sequence) and first-ever promo paired with Brandon, with Jimmy stepping in to demonstrate. Picks up corrections fast on the next rep.
